name: L Stability Condition for Linear Methods
statement_text:
A general linear method is L-stable if it is A-stable and
ρ(M (∞)) = 0.

variables.name: M(z)
variables.definition: the stability matrix of the general linear method, a function of the complex parameter z

variables.name: ρ(M(∞))
variables.definition: the spectral radius of M(z) as z → ∞

preamble: The discussion concerns stability properties of general linear methods for ODEs. A-stability is defined as power-boundedness of M(z) for all z in the left half-plane. L-stability is a stronger property that additionally requires the spectral radius to vanish at infinity.
